mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 6.5% signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a liquidity crisis regime, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals stabilizing supply chains and immediate positive market sentiment over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO MoM changes yield negative 5-day returns in more than 65% of crisis recovery months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5% while inflation remains above 2%, real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity discount rates and generating positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS MoM changes are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 1% annualized triggers immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative equity returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: GDP contractions below -1% are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling employment indicates capital substitution and margin expansion, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The divergence condition yields negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed months.
